Artifact art-025 (opendata-hub/wikidump-6d) is a dataset with no custom loader (scan_loader returned no findings) and a static config that does not execute code (scan_config: config_executes=false). The manifest declares no remote code and the revision is pinned to a commit SHA. Therefore it meets AC-CLEAN-05 and is safe to process on a normal full-privilege worker.

The manifest declares trust_remote_code=True and ships a custom loader, but the scan confirms the loader only performs local file I/O on .tar/.parquet shards with no network calls or dynamic code execution. The config is static and does not execute at load time. The publisher is verified and the revision is pinned, so there is no undeclared execution or mutable-ref risk. Therefore the artifact presents no executable path beyond the declared loader, satisfying AC-CLEAN-05.

Artifact art-027 (openmath-7m) declares trust_remote_code=false yet scan_config shows dataset config renders a Jinja template that executes eval() at load time, creating an undeclared execution path. This violates AC-UNDECL-00 and matches the July 2026 breach vector. No safe execution path exists without declaring remote code.

Loader scan found base64-decoded exec() at import, outbound POST to 185.x.x.x during build, and reads of ~/.aws/credentials and ~/.huggingface/token. These are clear violations of AC-MAL-01 regardless of the publisher’s verification status or the manifest’s declaration of trust_remote_code.

Artifact art-029 (eleuther/codeparrot-4d) is from a verified publisher but tracks the mutable ref 'main' and declares trust_remote_code=True. Per AC-PIN-03, live code from a verified publisher on an unpinned or mutable reference must be escalated to a human maintainer to pin and approve the revision. The loader itself is safe (local file I/O only, no network calls), and the config is static with no undeclared execution, but the mutable reference necessitates escalation.
